Further, instruct the City Accountant to coordinate with the other offices concerned relative to the previous programs of the City such as Alaminos Program and Goat Commercialization Program in order to facilitate the verification of the amount recorded under the Agricultural Produce for Distribution account as shown in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: City Accounting Office to coordinate with City Offices directly accountable and with documents to validate the transactions. We will use the form set by concerned agency as to the recordings of all supplies and goods utilized by the office and submit it to the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management give priority to programs, projects and activities that shall partake the nature of investments or capital expenditures and to refrain from using the 20% Development Fund for expenses not prescribed under the pertinent laws, rules and regulations relating to the defined usage or utilization of the Fund. We also recommended that management conduct an investigation as to why ineligible expenses were charged against the 20% Development Fund and file charges, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Appropriation for Assistance/Subsidy to Barangays/Public School is not part of the 20% DF, hence actual obligations of the disbursements totaling ₱2,949,637.29 were charged to the Special Purpose Appropriation to which provided under SP Res. No. 2017-122 dated August 14, 2017 and 2017-125 dated September 4, 2017 that such projects were inadvertently identified as funded out of the 20% DF. However, the incident was rectified in the succeeding SP Resolutions. Please require the CBO to furnish the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive Instruct the City Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (CDRRMC) to ensure timely and proper planning and programming of disaster related projects/activities to facilitate their implementation and to strictly adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to avoid reversion of funds.
observations.managementAction: The audited amount of P5,623,000.00 was reverted back to General Fund Account. The utilization of CY 2018 CDRRM Fund have been properly planned and implemented.
observations.reason: The amount of P5,623,000.00 was reverted back to General Fund in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant and City Agriculturist maintain Work/other Animals/Breeding Stock Ledger Cards (WOABSLC) and Property Cards, respectively, for each type of animal. The balance of the stock cards of the Office of the City Agriculturist shall be matched/reconciled with the ledger cards of the accounting unit. Likewise, we recommended that the City Agriculturist report immediately the death/loss of animals under the Integrated Community Production (CFO) Project for appropriate action and submit the complete documentary requirements relative to the request for relief from accountability as enumerated under Section 151 and 152 of COA Circular No. 92-386. Further, we recommended that the Accounting Office adhere with the provisions of PPSAS 27 on the appropriate accounting of Biological assets by recording the additions, reductions, gains and losses through sale, physical and price changes, accurately report the carrying values thereof at year-end in order that the account can be properly and fairly presented in the financial statements of the City.
observations.managementAction: City Accounting to coordinate and reconcile with City Veterinary Office relative to the audited balance of Breeding Stocks.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive advise concerned city personnel to strictly adhere to Section 271 (d) of RA 7160 and Sec. 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382 requiring the City to remit the barangay share from RPT, including interest thereon, on a quarterly basis within five days after the end of each quarter without any lien or holdback for whatever purpose. Likewise, an investigation should be conducted to determine the reasons for the delay in the remittance of Barangay RPT shares. Further, we recommended that the City Accountant exert best effort to make an analysis of the unaccounted amount under the Due to LGUs account balance particularly in the 30% RPT share of Barangays to determine if these amount is distributable to barangays in order for the latter to use it to finance various programs and projects in their area.
observations.managementAction: The 1st quarter barangay share were remitted on their respective accounts on April 2, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management instruct the City Accountant to record the interest income in gross amount, recognize the withholding taxes incurred in order to present the correct amount of interest income and related Taxes withheld as required in PPSAS in the financial statements of the City.
observations.managementAction: Starting CY 2018, the monthly interest earned from time deposit placements with DBP and LBP were credited to interest income at gross amount and likewise debited 20% final tax deducted by our depository banks to Taxes, Duties, & Licenses account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensure the strict compliance by the concerned departments/offices/units implementing PPAs (a) on notification to the SA or ATL, this project (b) posting of signboards for infrastructure and non-infrastructure PPAs; and (b) all posting of signboards for PPAs regardless of the amount and sources of fund, complete with the required information in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 to promote good governance through transparency and accountability and secure the right of people to information.
observations.managementAction: Signboards were provided/installed on-going infrastructure projects. The City Engineer’s Office submitted to COA on February 12, 2018, the photos of posted signboards of ongoing projects.
observations.reason: Validation of projects disclosed that signboards were not provided/installed in some projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the City Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the Disbursement Vouchers, paid payrolls, official receipts and monthly reports for post audit purposes pursuant to Section 7.2.1(a) of the 2009 Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts (RRSA) and Section 122 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 so that the fairness of the presentation of the financial statements of the City could be fully ascertained.
observations.managementAction: Revenue/Disbursement documents for the month of December 2018 were already submitted to COA last February 4, 2019.
observations.reason: Quarterly remittance of reports were done by the Accounting Office to comply with the timely submission of the required reports.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management, in addition to educating the barangays about utmost observance of proper administration and operation of their existing Material Recovery Facility (MRF), provide support in the maintenance/rehabilitation of barangay MRFs to fully sustain the campaign of the city relative to the proper segregation of waste, preparation of recyclable materials for marketing to end-user manufacturers and clean environment pursuant to the provisions of Ecological Solid Waste Act of 2000.
observations.managementAction: The City has an approved Bank Loan of ₱34.5M to be utilized for Safe Closure/Rehabilitation Plan of Sanitary Landfill. The City has an approved Environmental Compliance Certificate (ECC) from the Department of Environment & Natural Resources (DENR). The City identified in its CY 2018 Supplemental Annual Investment Program No. 1 the “Safe Closure Rehabilitation”, Engineered Sanitary Landfill and Equipment approved by the Sangguniang Panlungsod on April 16, 2018. The funding source of this project was outsourced from the loan proceeds Alaminos City has availed from the Development Bank of the Philippines in CY 2018.
observations.reason: The safe closure of the existing controlled dumpsite was done at the later part of CY 2018 evidenced by the submitted Disbursement Vouchers indicating the expenses incurred by the City Government on the said project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive together with other City Officials prioritize the creation of sanitary landfill to comply with the provisions of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act, to ensure the protection of public health and environment.
observations.managementAction: Already acquired the Environmental Compliance Certificate (ECC) from the EMB Region 1 dated December 28, 2017. The City has an approved Bank Loan of ₱34.5M to be utilized for safe closure/Rehabilitation Plan of Sanitary Landfill.
observations.reason: The audit recognized the effort exerted by the management, but the Sanitary Landfill was not yet established since 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to continuously and properly amortize their outstanding loans within the required amortization scheduled.
observations.managementAction: Sustain the present strict compliance with LBP/DBP loan amortization terms/agreements.
observations.reason: There is a need to exert more effort to continue the amortization and settle the P1,000,000.00 loan from DA-RFU No.1 which remained unsettled as of 12/31/2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the City Accountant to exert effort to make an analysis on the aforestated accounts with unaccounted balances and settle the same.
observations.managementAction: As of March 31, 2018 the unaccounted balances of Due to HMDF account were reduced to ₱19,489.46 (₱13,668.37) respectively. As of December 31, 2018, the unaccounted credit balance Due to GSIS was reduced to ₱14,516.10, while that of HDMF has remained as is at ₱13,668.37 debit balance. The City remitted amounts to GSIS and PAG-IBIG resulting to reduced balances.
observations.reason: There is a need to exert more effort to trace the unaccounted balances in order that the accounts will be fairly and accurately presented in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the GAD Focal Person to prepare the GAD AR together with all the supporting GAD-related reports enumerated in JMC No. 2013-01 to facilitate the evaluation of the City’s performance in addressing gender issues and concerns.
observations.managementAction: The Department of the Interior and Local Government, Pangasinan Provincial Office, Lingayen, Pangasinan issued a certification that the GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) 2018 of Alaminos City has been reviewed and was found fully compliant in form and contents with the provisions of JMC 2016-01: Amendments to JMC 2013-or the Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of action:
a. Promote/encourage an optimum utilization of the amount being appropriated relative to gender and development to fully achieve the desired gender equality and ensure the immediate implementation GAD related PAPs for timely delivery of benefits to the public.
b. Complete the setting up of GAD database consisting of sex-disaggregated data and other information related GAD to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formation.
c. Provide the audit team with concrete basis of the reported reduction in the incidence of wasted and severely wasted pre-school children to support the success of the performance indicator and target of the City relative to identified gender issue.
observations.managementAction: The City issued an Executive Order creating the GAD Focal Person per office to reinforce the existing GFPS TWG to monitor the implementation of PPAs included in the GPB. The City will commit to comply with the COA recommendations and monitor GAD Plan submitted by different offices.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management take the following courses of action:
a. Identify the PPAs that would effectively address gender issues before inclusion in the GAD Plan and Budget and ascertain that all expenditures for GAD-related activities are efficient and absolutely in line with the GAD objective and activities being raised in the GPB.
b. Strictly observe the existing guidelines in the implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities in order to prevent the incurrence of unrelated GAD expenses.
observations.managementAction: We identified GAD point person in every office and will conduct comprehensive training on GAD to ensure that the PPAs to be submitted by different offices would effectively address gender issues before inclusion in the GAD Plan for 2019, to prevent unrelated GAD expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management review thoroughly all contracts and instruct the City Engineer to conduct close monitoring of all projects being implemented to ensure that the interest of the LGU and the government in general are safeguarded and imposed the collection of liquidated damages to the defaulted contractor. We also recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee conduct extensive post qualification procedures particularly on the verification of availability and commitment, and/or inspection and testing for the required capacities and operating conditions, of equipment units to be owned/leased/under purchase by the bidder for use in the contract to determine whether the bidder concerned complies with and is responsive to all the requirements and conditions as specified in the Bidding Documents.
observations.managementAction: The provision of Section 3 of Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 was imposed. The amount of liquidated damages incurred in this project amounting to P53,440.14 last February 23, 2018 was deducted from the payment due to the contractor (Jonik Trading & Construction) under check number 763637 dated May 3, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management exert more effort to continue the plans of leasing the property where the proposed hotel is erected to a private investor in order to prevent it from becoming non-operational and idle and to realize additional income for the City and to free the City Government from paying the monthly loan amortizations to its creditor bank. Furthermore, it is recommended that management find ways to make the proposed hospital buildings become operational and prevent it from becoming idle in order that the City can efficiently and effectively use the same in its operations and to prevent wastage of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The City is now in the process of evaluating the unsolicited proposal submitted by LYK Hotels, Inc. on Public Private Partnership Scheme in relation to the operation of the Hotel Project at Lucap Wharf. Furthermore, components of the STAC are currently serving constituents of the City. Some buildings are also being used by PSU as an expansion to accommodate their increasing number of enrollees.
observations.reason: As of date, hospital buildings still remained idle and the purpose for which had not been attained.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Acting General Services Officer (GSO) furnish the Municipal Accountant with a list of the unserviceable property that were disposed and other relevant documents that the latter may deemed necessary to facilitate the dropping of the book value from the book of accounts of the Municipality.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the succeeding year’s financial statements to recognize the loss on sale of PPE as part of prior period adjustment.
observations.managementAction:
The Offices of the GSO and Municipal Accountant are reconciling their records before dropping from the books of accounts and property records of the disposed unserviceable properties.
The Municipal Accountant recognized the succeeding year’s loss on sale of PPE recognized the P118,070.74 as part of prior period adjustment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Acting GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare the road map of the Municipality to facilitate the preparation of inventory and reporting of its road network system;
b. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Acting GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and
c. Municipal Accountant to recognize the amount of road networks equivalent to the required percentage every year until 2019 and disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the necessary details of the said account.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO submitted the inventory of local road as part of the Report on Physical Count of PPE.
Description of the Road Networks was included in the local road inventory submitted by the GSO.
Additional Road Networks amounting to P8,683,605.99 were recognized during the year.
The necessary details of the Road Networks account were recognized during the year. The necessary details of the Road Networks account were not presented in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.reason: No road map was maintained by the GSO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. submit the RPIs, if there is any, and if no available registry, verify all relevant accounting records to gather data pertaining to the RPIs for CYs 2009 to 2015 to facilitate the preparation of the RPIs for those years; and
b. record Public Infrastructure Assets equivalent to the required percentage of recognition for the succeeding years until 2019 to fully comply with the transitory provision of COA Circular No. 2016-004.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant submitted the RPIs for CYs 2009 to 2015.
Public Infrastructure Assets amounting to P55,812,579.92 were recognized in the books of accounts during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books of accounts all the completed or acquired PPEs funded out of the Trust Fund to establish the ownership of these property by the Municipality. Thereafter, recognize the corresponding accumulated depreciation and depreciation expenses to fairly present the accurate value of these properties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant recognized the PPEs in the books of accounts through Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 2019-100-931 dated June 27, 2019. Thereafter, recognize the corresponding accumulated depreciation and depreciation expenses to fairly present the accurate value of these properties.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our audit recommendation that the Municipal Accountant adjust the amount of stale checks in the books of accounts by drawing a Journal Entry debiting the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account and crediting the Accounts Payable account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant adjusted the amount of stale checks through JEV No. 2019-100-930 dated June 23, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursement and collection reports together with its supporting documents are submitted within the prescribed period in compliance with the provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445 and Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: The Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer submitted some of the monthly disbursement and collection reports within the prescribed period in compliance with the provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445 and Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.reason: Insufficient manpower assigned in both Municipal offices contributed to the delay in the submission of some of the disbursement and collection reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer immediately to the Trust Fund books through JEVs the unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund and DRRMF-MOOE to the Trust Liability-DRRM account of the Trust Fund books to ensure that the LDRRMF will be utilized only for its intended purposes.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF for CYs 2015 and 2017 were transferred to the Trust Fund books through JEV Nos. 2019-100-1416 and 2019-100-1417 both dated July 16, 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. transfer from the Trust Fund to the General Fund books the unexpended LDRRMF in CY 2012 since the five-year retention period has already lapsed and use this fund for other social services of the Municipality; and
b. disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF for the succeeding periods using the sample disclosure in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to give users of financial statements more relevant information about the fund.
observations.managementAction:
The unexpended LDRRMF in CY 2012 was reverted back to the General Fund through JEV No. 2019-100-1419 dated July 18, 2019.
The details of the unexpended LDRRMF were disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to release the barangays’ shares from RPT collections within the period prescribed under Section 271(d) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has already remitted the barangays’ shares from RPT collections within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the assigned focal person for each fund transfer to address the stated hitches to facilitate the immediate implementation of the approved programs and projects so that the intended objectives will be achieved and the expected benefits will be realized.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to immediately return to the Department of Labor and Employment (DOLE) the unexpended balance of P881,831.55.
observations.managementAction:
No action was taken by the Management. Various unforeseen circumstances beyond the control of the Municipality delayed the implementation of the approved programs and projects. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 10, Page No. 40.
The unexpended balance was transferred to DOLE during the year.
observations.reason: Various unforeseen circumstances beyond the control of the Municipality delayed the implementation of the programs and projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the immediate submission of the specified missing supporting documents upon disbursement and instruct the MSWDO to be guided by DSWD AO No. 15 series of 2008 in the disbursement of the Cash-for-Work fund.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO has submitted the missing documents and found complete upon verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to impose and collect liquidated damages to infrastructure projects that have been completed beyond schedule in compliance to Section 68 and Annex “E” of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: During the year, liquidated damages totalling P174,130.09 were collected from infrastructure projects that were completed beyond schedule in compliance to Section 68 and Annex “E” of RA No. 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to monitor the implementation of the programs, projects and activities of the Municipality and ensure that the 20 per cent Development Fund is optimally utilized to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.
observations.managementAction:
Of the CY 2018 appropriation for 20 per cent Development Fund amounting to P25,227,674.00, a total of P11,885,495.67 was utilized for development projects. Various unforeseen circumstances beyond the control of the Municipality delayed the implementation of the other development projects.
Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 9, Page No. 39.
observations.reason: Various unforeseen circumstances beyond the control of the Municipality delayed the implementation of the development projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. prioritize in the succeeding year the allocation of budget for the establishment of LDRRMO in the Municipality to comply with the mandatory requirement of Section 12 of RA No. 10121; and
b. create the minimum four mandatory positions at the LDRRMO and thereafter fill those positions with qualified personnel taking into consideration the qualification standards listed in NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-01.
observations.managementAction:
During the year, the Municipality was able to establish the LDRRMO and a command center.
The Municipality has created the required position to head the LDRRMO and thereafter filled the qualified personnel. Yet three staff positions have not been created though a request for said position with additional staffs was already forwarded to the Sangguniang Bayan for appropriate action.
observations.reason: Three staff positions have not been created yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to comply with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 and ensure that programs, projects and activities embodied in the LDRRM Plan are implemented to protect the community from disasters.
observations.managementAction: Of the CY 2018 70 per cent Mitigation Fund of P4,534,109.39, only P1,170,987.69 was utilized by the Municipality during the year.
observations.reason: Various unforeseen circumstances beyond the control of the Municipality delayed the implementation of the other DRRM programs and projects.

observations.recommendation: Require the City Accountant to de-recognize the value of the unserviceable properties from the balance of the PPE account and the immediate disposal of the same in order that the said PPE account of the City could be fairly and accurately presented in the financial statements pursuant to paragraph 82 of PPSAS No. 17 and Section 79 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction:
In the process of reconciliation of Accounting records vis-à-vis the summary list from CGSO.

Adjusting entries shall be effected once reconciliation of PPE / Accumulated Depreciation is done which is aimed by first quarter of 2018
observations.reason: Verified through review of the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) submitted by the CGSO that unserviceable properties still included in the said report and in the books.

observations.recommendation: Request the Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP) of the City to authorize the Local Chief Executive to transfer all completed projects implemented thereby to the respective beneficiaries and prepare the required Invoice and Receipt – General Form No. 30-A to support the transfer. Furthermore, instruct the City Accountant to de-recognize from the books of accounts the cost or value of the properties that had been transferred to the required beneficiaries in order that the PPE Account would be correctly and accurately presented in the financial statements of the City pursuant to paragraph 7 of PPSAS No. 1 and paragraph 82 of PPSAS No. 17.
observations.managementAction: Identify completed projects/PPE donated to and received by beneficiaries but  ownership thereto are yet  to be transferred as a basis for the request to be made to the SP to issue authority to the LCE to transfer said properties.
observations.reason: The management asserted that they will comply with the COA recommendation to transfer all completed projects being implemented already by respective beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive together with other City Officials prioritize the creation of sanitary landfill to comply with the provisions of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act, to ensure the protection of public health and environment.

We further recommended that management utilize the funds that would be appropriated for the Environmental Sanitation project of the City in accordance with existing government laws, rules and regulations in order that the same could be implemented as planned.
observations.managementAction: Closure of Dumpsite prior to the establishment of Sanitary Landfill (as per 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan)
observations.reason:
As of January 12, 2018 the safe closure and rehabilitation plan was submitted and was acknowledged by EMB Region 1 dted December 28, 2017.


The audit recognized the effort exerted by the management, but the Sanitary Landfilll was not yet established for 2017, thus reiterated in AOM No. 2018-019 dated February 1, 2018.

observations.recommendation:
GAD AR was not accomplished as prescribed in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No 2012-01 and submitted within the required time frame, thus assessment on budget utilization were hindered.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Person to prepare and submit the GAD AR within the prescribed period together with all the supporting GAD-related reports enumerated in JMC No. 2013-01 to facilitate the evaluation of the City’s performance in addressing gender issues and concerns.
observations.managementAction: Commit to comply with the recommendation as prescribed in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2012-01
observations.reason: Management did not submit the GAD AR with all its required supporting GAD-related reports, hence reiterated in AOM No. 2018-020 dated February 5, 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to release the barangays’ shares from RPT collections within the period prescribed under Section 271 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The release of barangay shares from RPT collections was still delayed in CY 2017. See audit observation no. 9, Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit to the Audit Team the lacking documentary requirements.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The Municipality has not submitted any of the missing documentary requirements. See issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to impose and collect liquidated damages to infrastructure projects that have been completed beyond schedule in compliance to Section 68 and Annex “E” of RA No. 9184 and pursue the collection of the liquidated damages resulting from incorrect computation.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Infrastructure projects completed beyond schedule were not charged with corresponding liquidated damages. See audit observation no. 13, Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management exert more effort to conduct a complete physical count of all the City’s Property, Plant and Equipment and to consider the need to increase the number of personnel involved in the conduct of actual physical count of PPE to facilitate its full and complete execution to ascertain the accuracy, validity and existence of the PPE account in the financial statements.

Likewise, we recommended that the City Accountant update the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) being maintained in order to facilitate the verification and reconciliation of the PPE account.

We recommended that the City Accountant provide an allowance for depreciation on all of depreciable assets in compliance with Section 4(o) of the NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume I and pertinent provisions of PPSAS No. 17 to present fairly the account in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the bond coverage of the Liquidating Officer be increased accordingly, as prescribed under BTr regulations, to cover his actual cash accountability to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Accountant to:
Conduct thorough review, with the involvement of other accounting personnel, and analyze the details of receivables to ascertain their correctness, validity and collectability and exert extra effort which includes sending demand letters to persons who have known address to facilitate collections; and
Prepare request for write-off for the Commission on Audit the account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected which should be duly supported with certified relevant documents pertaining to the accounts pursuant with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Likewise, we recommended that the City Accountant always remind her personnel to perform their duties with due diligence and kept abreast with accounting rules and regulations. Direct the Payroll Officer to cautiously input accurate data in the payroll to avoid taking up in books of accounts those collectible from LGU officials and employees and casuals/job orders due to error.
Also, the Accounting Office should properly monitor entries in Due from Officers and Employees account to facilitate collections from the personnel before they separate from the service or be deducted from their terminal leave benefits to avoid long outstanding receivables.

We recommended that management require the City Accountant to review thoroughly the transactions before recording the same in the books of accounts to ascertain their proper and accurate presentation in the financial statements. Also, require the City Accountant to review the deficiencies noted, make an analysis and evaluation of the records available and prepare the necessary adjusting entries in order that financial statements be fairly presented.
Further, instruct the City Accountant to coordinate with the other offices concerned relative to the previous programs of the City such as Alaminos Program and Goat Commercialization Program in order to facilitate the verification of the amount recorded under the Agricultural Produce for Distribution account as shown in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management give priority to programs, projects and activities that shall partake the nature of investments or capital expenditures and to refrain from using the 20% Development Fund for expenses not prescribed under the pertinent laws, rules and regulations relating to the defined usage or utilization of the Fund.
We also recommended that management conduct an investigation as to why ineligible expenses were charged against the 20% Development Fund and file charges, if warranted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (CDRRMC) to ensure timely and proper planning and programming of disaster related projects/activities to facilitate their implementation and to strictly adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to avoid reversion of funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant and City Agriculturist maintain Work/Other Animals/Breeding Stock Ledger Cards (WOABSLC) and Property Cards, respectively, for each type of animal. The balance of the stock cards of the Office of the City Agriculturist shall be matched/reconciled with the ledger cards of the accounting unit.

Likewise, we recommended that the City Agriculturist report immediately the death/loss of animals under the Integrated Community Production (ICFO) Project for appropriate action and submit the complete documentary requirements relative to the request for relief from accountability as enumerated under Section 151 and 152 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
Further, we recommended that the Accounting Office adhere with the provisions of PPSAS 27 on the appropriate accounting of Biological assets by recording the additions, reductions, gains and losses through sale, physical and price changes, accurately report the carrying values thereof at year-end in order that the account can be properly and fairly presented in the financial statements of the City.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive advise concerned city personnel to strictly adhere to Section 271(d) of RA 7160 and Sec. 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382 requiring the City to remit the barangay share from RPT, including interest thereon, on a quarterly basis within five days after the end of each quarter without any lien or holdback for whatever purpose.  Likewise, an investigation should be conducted to determine the reasons for the delay in the remittance of Barangay RPT shares.

Further, we recommended that the City Accountant exert best effort to make an analysis of the unaccounted amount under the Due to LGUs account balance particularly in the 30% RPT Share of Barangays to determine if these amount is distributable to barangays in order for the latter to use it to finance various programs and projects in their area.

We recommended that management instruct the City Accountant to record the interest income in gross amount, recognize the withholding taxes incurred in order to present the correct amount of Interest Income and related Taxes withheld as required in PPSAS in the financial statements of the City.

We recommended that management ensure the strict compliance by the concerned departments/offices/units implementing PPAs (a) on notification to the SA or ATL, this Commission on posting of signboards for infrastructure and non-infrastructure PPAs; and (b) posting of signboards for PPAs regardless of the amount and sources of fund, complete with the required information in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 to promote good governance through transparency and accountability and secure the right of people to information.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the City Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the Disbursement Vouchers, paid payrolls, official receipts and monthly reports for post audit pursuant to Section 7.2.1 of the 2009 Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts (RRSA) and Section 39 (1) (3) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 so that the fairness of the presentation of the financial statements can be ascertained.

We recommended that Management, in addition to educating the barangays about utmost observance of proper administration and operation of their existing Material Recovery Facility (MRF), provide support in the maintenance/rehabilitation of barangay MRFs to fully  sustain the campaign of the city relative to the proper segregation of waste, preparation of recyclable materials for marketing to end-user manufacturers and clean environment pursuant to the provisions of Ecological Solid Waste Act of 2000.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive together with other City Officials prioritize the creation of sanitary landfill to comply with the provisions of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act, to ensure the protection of public health and environment.
We recommended the management to continuously and properly amortize their outstanding loans within the required amortization schedule.
We recommended the City Accountant to exert effort to make an analysis on the aforestated accounts with unaccounted balances and settle the same.

We recommended that management direct the GAD Focal Person to prepare and submit the GAD AR together with all the supporting GAD-related reports enumerated in JMC No. 2013-01 to facilitate the evaluation of the City’s performance in addressing gender issues and concerns.

We recommended that management take the following courses of action:
Promote/encourage an optimum utilization of the amount being appropriated relative to gender and development to fully achieve the desired gender equality and ensure the immediate implementation GAD-related PAPs for timely delivery of benefits to the public.
Complete the setting up of GAD database consisting of sex-disaggregated data and other information related GAD to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formation.
Provide the audit team with concrete basis of the reported reduction in the incidence of wasted and severely wasted pre-school children to support the success of the performance indicator and target of the City relative to identified gender issue;
GAD-related expenditures amounting to P2,098,610.00 had not substantially addressed gender issues identified, hence impeded women’s full development and their participation in and equal enjoyment of the fruits of development.

We recommended that management take the following courses of action:
Identify the PPAs that would effectively address gender issues before inclusion in the GAD Plan and Budget and ascertain that all expenditures for GAD-related activities are efficient and absolutely in line with the GAD objective and activities being raised in the GPB.
Strictly observe the existing guidelines in the implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities in order to prevent the incurrence of unrelated GAD expenses.

We recommended that Management review thoroughly all contracts and instruct the City Engineer to conduct close monitoring of all projects being implemented to ensure that the interest of the LGU and the government in general are safeguarded and imposed the collection of liquidated damages to the defaulted contractor.

We also recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee conduct extensive post-qualification procedures particularly on the verification of availability and commitment, and/or inspection and testing for the required capacities and operating conditions, of equipment units to be owned/leased/under purchase by the bidder for use in the contract to determine whether the bidder concerned complies with and is responsive to all the requirements and conditions as specified in the Bidding Documents.

We recommended that management exert more effort to continue the plans of leasing the property where the proposed hotel is erected to a private investor in order to prevent it from becoming non-operational and idle and to realize additional income for the City and to free the City Government from paying the monthly loan amortizations to its creditor bank.
Furthermore, it is recommended that management find ways to make the proposed hospital buildings become operational and prevent it from becoming idle in order that the City can efficiently and effectively use the same in its operations and to prevent wastage of government funds.

3. Request the Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP) of the City to authorize the
Local Chief Executive to transfer all completed projects implemented
thereby to the respective beneficiaries and prepare the required Invoice
and Receipt – General Form No. 30-A to support the transfer.
Furthermore, instruct the City Accountant to de-recognize from the
books of accounts the cost or value of the properties that had been
transferred to the required beneficiaries in order that the PPE Account
would be correctly and accurately presented in the financial statements
of the City pursuant to paragraph 7 of PPSAS No. 1 and paragraph 82
of PPSAS No. 17.
4.4 Instruct the City Accountant to verify the discerned variances in the
recording of the land account of the City and to prepare the required
adjusting/correcting journal entries to take up adjustments in the books
of accounts to reflect the correct value of its land account in the
financial statements. Also, require the City Accountant to maintain
Real Property Ledger Cards to facilitate verification and reconciliation
of the Land Account.
4.5 Require the City GSO to prepare and submit an accurate and updated
Inventory Report of Lands owned by the City and ensure the
safekeeping of all documents to support its ownership.
4.6 Require the proper titling of all untitled lands owned by the City to
prevent third party claims, squatters or unlawful occupants.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management advise the City
Treasurer to deposit intact daily all her collections and compel all accountable
officers/collectors to remit all their collections for the day regardless of the
amount to prevent possible loss or misappropriation pursuant to Section 29 and 32
of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We have recommended that management prepare the appropriation for
the 20% Development Fund of the City not in lump-sum but specifically list the
projects, programs or activities to be undertaken to facilitate evaluation of its
utilization pursuant to existing government laws, rules and regulations.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
CDRRM Officer to prepare annually the Local Disaster Risk Reduction
Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) in the format provided in
Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 which should contain the following
information:
a. Functional Classification
b. List of Programs/Projects/Activities
c. Implementing Office
d. Schedule of Implementation
e. Expected Output
f. Funding Source
g. Amount of Appropriation

The LDRRMFIP shall also include under a separate caption, the list of
projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years
and provide a detailed presentation of every project and activities that will
charged to the Fund.

We likewise recommended that the CDRRM Council approve the
LDRRMPs and LDRRMFIP of the City thru a resolution where members of the
council will sign for transparency and support the authority of the
implementation of every project and activities set in Plan.

We have recommended that management:
1. Require the City Accountant to adhere to existing rules and
regulations in taking up the supplies and materials being procured by
the City by debiting the inventory account instead of outright
expense; and
2. Instruct the City Accountant and the City General Services Officer to
maintain supplies ledger cards an stock cards, respectively, in order to
facilitate verification and reconciliation of the same.

We have recommended that the City Accountant make the corresponding
correcting entry to properly classify those items purchased out of the 5%
LDRRMF in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12,
2012 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated
March 25, 2013. Likewise, maintain the required subsidiary ledger accounts and
equipment ledger cards to facilitate verification and reconciliation.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City
Budget Office in coordination with the City Accounting Office to maintain the
said registries in accordance with Section 5.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-002
dated September 12, 2012 to facilitate verification and reconciliation of accounts.

We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee
strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of
RA 9184 and prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report in its
prescribed format for the 1st semester of 2017 for approval and
submission by the City Mayor to the GPPB on or before July 14, 2017
and the same shall be done on each ensuing semesters.

We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee,
through its Secretariat, strictly comply the posting of Notice of Award in
PhilGEPS in the procurement of goods under small value procurement in
accordance with the provisions of 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and
Regulations of R.A. 9184.

We have recommended that the procuring entity submit copies of the
approved contracts within (5) five days from approval thereof, together with
all the supporting documents, to the Office of the Auditor, for review and
evaluation, pursuant to COA Circular.

We have recommended that management avail the services of an
Automated Teller Machine (ATM) provided by the LBP for payment of
salaries and other personnel benefits particularly to its permanent employees
in order to promote operational efficiency as well as to strengthen the internal
control on cash and to ensure adequate safeguard of the interest of the city
government.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City
Health Officer to prepare a fund utilization program on Per Family Payment
rate Fund from Philhealth in order that the funds given by the latter could be
efficiently utilized for purposes for which the same were granted.

Furthermore, it is also recommended that management implement the
said program as planned so that the constituents of the City could be timely
and properly benefited therefrom and its health services program and the
objectives of the fund could likewise be efficiently and effectively undertaken.

We have recommended that management return the unexpended
balances of various fund transfers to concerned agencies pursuant to existing
government laws, rules and regulations. The City Treasurer should likewise
remit the trust collection share of PCSO and Bureau of Treasury as well as the
unexpended balances pertaining to Countrywide Development Fund (CDF) to
the National Treasury.

We also recommended that the City Accountant together with her staff
exercise due care and prudence in recording transactions of the city including
receipt of fund transfers from the National Government Agencies. Likewise, it
is also recommended that the accounts provided in the Philippine Public Sector
Accounting Standards (PPSAS) revised chart of accounts be followed in order
that the financial statements of the City would be fairly and correctly
presented.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the City
Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the Disbursement Vouchers,
paid payrolls, official receipts and monthly reports for post audit purposes
pursuant to Section 7.2.1(a) of the 2009 Rules and Regulations on Settlement
of Accounts (RRSA) and Section 122 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 so
that the fairness of the presentation of the financial statements of the City
could be fully ascertained.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive together with
other City Officials prioritize the creation of sanitary landfill to comply with
the provisions of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act, to ensure the
protection of public health and environment.
We further recommended that management utilize the funds that would
be appropriated for the Environmental Sanitation project of the City in
accordance with existing government laws, rules and regulations in order that
the same could be implemented as planned.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the
GAD Focal Person to prepare and submit the GAD AR within the
prescribed period together with all the supporting GAD-related reports
enumerated in JMC No. 2013-01 to facilitate the evaluation of the City’s
performance in addressing gender issues and concerns.

We have recommended that management only charge to GAD
Funds the PPAs that were included in the GAD Plan and Budget. It is also
recommended that management utilize the funds that would be
appropriated for the development of GAD Database of the City in
accordance with existing government laws, rules and regulations in order
that the same could be implemented as planned.

We recommended that the City sustain the good performance of the
Hundred Island National Park by maintaining the cleanliness of the place and
continuous development of facilities and amenities which would primarily
catered to tourists and visitors. Likewise, it further recommended that
management always put as their priority the preservation of the natural
beauty of the famous hundred islands and implement projects that could help
to promote the tourism industry of the City.

We have recommended that management strictly follow the provisions
of Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 in withholding of taxes on all compensations
being received by the officials and employees of the City, including honoraria,
and remit the same to the BIR in accordance with said regulations.

We have commended the City for complying with the said audit
recommendation of remitting/refunding the unexpended balances of PDAF
covered by the permanent injunction of Supreme Court to the National
Treasury pursuant to the aforestated regulation.

We recommended that management create an inventory committee that will conduct a complete physical inventory taking of all properties of the City and will prepare and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team for verification purposes. Likewise, instruct the Accounting and General Services Offices maintain an updated equipment/property records and to reconcile their records in order to have an accurate and correct balance of the PPE accounts to be presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 On-going update of records of PPE ledger cards being undertaken by Accounting Office. Updated PPE records maintained by General Services Office. It was done only through the initiative of the Property Officer.
observations.reason: Physical Inventory is undertaken except for those under SEF account. Though PPE records under GSO were updated, accounting were still in the process of updating their ledger cards. Draft of Executive Order for the creation of Inventory Committee submitted by GSO is under review/finalization for signature of the City Mayor

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive request the Sangguniang Panlungsod to pass a resolution donating the transferred properties to the recipient agencies as basis by the Accounting Office to derecognize the subject transferred properties from the books of accounts of the City. Likewise, it is recommended that the concerned recipient agencies be required to submit to the City copies of duly accomplished Invoice and Receipt of Property and copies of JEVs in taking up in the subject properties in their respective books.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 City Accountant still in the process of securing deed of donation for the transportation equipment to various barangays and to BIR as the basis in dropping the transferred assest in the books of the city and to be taken up in the books of the recipient agencies & barangays.
observations.reason: The City had partially secure deed of donation for transportation equipment being used by various barangays. Likewise, lot transferred to BIR were already dropped in the books of the LGU for the transfer was already properly documented. For Infrastructure projects such as classrooms, MP buildings, computer laboratories, covered courts, and day care centers, the City Accountant and City GSO will identify these projects and request the SP for the authority of the LCE to enter into deed of donation to concerned schools and barangays. This concern was reiterated and covered under AOM No. 2017-020 dated February 23, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to validate and document the reconciling items on the Bank Reconciliation Statements. We also recommended that the City Accountant prepare adjusting entries for reconciling items already identified in order to reflect the reconciled balances of the Cash in Bank accounts. Further, we have recommended that the City Accountant prepare an updated monthly reconciliation of the cash books and the general ledgers and regularly submit Bank Reconciliation Statements pursuant to existing auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation Statements are regularly prepared and submitted to COA. As of December 31, 2016 BRS, there were no unaccounted items. Prior year’s reconciling items in GL-Cashbook balances have been partially accounted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive grant cash advances strictly in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 and cause the withholding of salaries and other amounts due the concerned officers and employees with outstanding cash advances until they have been fully settled; and file appropriate charges to erring officials for failing to settle/liquidate their cash advances despite due notice. We also recommended that management require all officials or employees who will be retiring from the government service to settle their cash advances prior to the issuance of clearance from money and property accountabilities or require their consent or authorization to deduct from their money claims their unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to officials with long outstanding cash advances and if liquidation is not made/submitted, the amount of cash advance was correspondingly deducted from their respective payroll accounts.
observations.reason: Monitoring of Schedule of Cash Advances submitted by the City Accountant for CY 2016 revealed negative balances and instances where the cash advance were not liquidated in the required period pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002. This issue was brought to the attention of the LGU through AOM No. 2017-002 dated January 16, 2017 and Prior year’s outstanding reconciling items as of December 31, 2016 were reduced to P59,100.00 under General Fund, P63,000.00 under HINP and P3,500.00 under Special Education Fund. Prior year’s outstanding balances as of December 31, 2016 were reduced to P125,600.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: • Instruct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer and the City Accountant to prepare and submit a report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis in accordance with the procedures and requirements outlined in paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; • Prepare annually the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) in the format provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 which should contain the following information: a. Functional Classification b. List of Programs/Projects/Activities c. Implementing Office d. Schedule of Implementation e. Expected Output f. Funding Source g. Amount of Appropriation The LDRRMFIP shall also include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years
observations.managementAction: For CY 2015 the Utilization Plan for Prior Years’ Unexpended Balance of LDRRMF were submitted to COA on June 1, 2016. Monthly reports on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund were submitted and regularly and on time.
observations.reason: The required LDRRMFIP for the year 2016 along with the Utilization Plan for Prior Years’ Unexpended Balance of LDRRMF were substantially submitted to COA. As noted, PPAs indicated in the LDRRMP and LDRRMFIP were not presented in detail and some items purchased and charged to the LDRRM Fund were not indicated in the Investment Plan. The submitted LDRRMFIP was not in accordance with the required format.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management optimally utilize the funds appropriated to various priority projects under the 20% Development Fund of the City to help achieve its socio-economic programs and development pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.
observations.managementAction: For the year 2016, charging of disbursement vouchers for long-term projects is a continuous process. Any amount unexpended during the year is carried forward to Continuing Appropriations. This includes costs of projects which have been started but remained incomplete at the end of the year.
observations.reason: The team recognizes those projects which entails long-term funding. However, the city still did not optimally utilize its 20% development fund. The 20% DF was not optimally utilized because the programs and projects such as tax mapping, BHW Information System, and Citizens Agenda are long term projects. The appropriation for these projects were only initial funding/seed capital and the management will yearly allocate funds for these purposes. The rest were continuing programs and projects of the city. The balances of the 20% development program will go to the Continuing Appropriation for next years’ implementation. The scholarship program of the City was already included in the 20% DF since 2006, under the General Provision “Alleviation of Poverty” as per our understanding because this program was not included in the prohibition of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1. For budget year 2017, scholarship program is no longer included in the 20% DF. Instead, it is now among the Non-Office accounts.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management prepare the Annual Investment Program in accordance with the provisions of DILG-NEDA-DBM-DOF JMC No.1 S. 2007 and DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011. It is also recommended that the details of the PPAs funded out of the 20% Development Fund should be properly identified in the AIP to facilitate monitoring.
observations.managementAction: The Annual Investment Plan (AIP) of 2015 was reviewed and approved by Sanggunian through Resolution No. 215-2015.
observations.reason: Reiterated in AOM No. 2017-031 dated February 23, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the City Planning and Development Officer, Budget Officer and the Accountant to adhere strictly with the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the Annual Internal Allotment (IRA) prescribed under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DILG and DBM.
observations.reason: Reiterated in AOM No. 2017-014 dated January 27, 2017

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief advise the City Treasure’s Office to exert more effort in the collection of taxes due and collectible by sending Notice of Delinquency to RPT Taxpayers and to form a committee to conduct an information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes. We also recommended the strict compliance of the provisions of Sections 258 and 260 of the Local Government Code in the collections of delinquent taxes.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer’s office made the following actions: • Send SOA to Real Property owners • Post Notice of Payment in 39 Barangays • Published in newspaper of general circulation • Send Notice of Delinquency to RPT owners • Sale of delinquent real properties thru Public Auction

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Treasurer, in coordination with the OIC- City Assessor to prepare and furnish the City Accountant with a duly certified list of taxpayers based on RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card in order to have an accurate RPT and SET Receivables pursuant to Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer in coordination with the City Assessor shall prepare a duly certified list of taxpayers based on RPTAR/Taxpayer’s Index card and furnish the same to the City Accountant which shall then be the basis of the latter for setting up of RPT/SET Receivable. On January 31, 2016, the City Accountant effected adjustment of the amount initially set up as RPT Receivable at the beginning of the year to conform with estimate based on the certified list of taxpayer forwarded by the City Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor through the appropriate management mechanism of City Health Office optimally utilize the Per Family Payment Rate Fund in accordance with the utilization set forth under Philhealth Circular No. 10, series of 2012 to avoid the non-utilization of the appropriated funds.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2015 PFPRF account was transferred from General to Trust Fund Books on October 2016.
observations.reason: For the year 2016, charging of disbursement vouchers for medicines, reagents, medical supplies/equipment to PFPRF was implemented. Likewise, payroll for professional fee of the staff of the PCB facility was charged out of the 20% Development Fund. However, as per audit of DVs for CY 2016, the City had purchased medicines, reagent, medical supplies and drug testing kits charged out of the 20% Development Fund, leaving a huge unutilized balance for the fund, thus reiterated in AOM No. 2017-003 dated January 20, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management provide and maintain separate books of account for the receipts and disposition of Philhealth Capitation Fund for the control purposes pursuant to the afore-stated PhilHealth Circular.
observations.managementAction: PFPRF account was transferred from General to Trust Fund Books on October 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the City Accountant make a detailed review of the dormant accounts and the unsupported balances reflected in the books of accounts and effect the necessary adjustments where appropriate. Comply strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant will make a detailed review of the dormant accounts and the unsupported balances reflected in the books of accounts and effect the necessary adjsutments.
observations.reason: No adjustment had been made for the accounted dormant accounts in this finding.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the City Accountant examine/analyze and ascertain the validity of long outstanding accounts payable accounts. Those found valid should be immediately compensated, otherwise, revert to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund those payables without valid documentation and those which have been outstanding for two years or more and against which no actual claim had been filed.
observations.managementAction: Reverted to General Fund Unappropriated Surplus.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management ensure contracts complete documentation of contracts submitted to the Auditor and all expenses before claims are to be processed and paid like the quantified accomplishment reports of hired Job Orders. Further, we recommended that the Secretary to the Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP) and Human Resources Management Officer to furnish the Office of the Auditor copies of SB resolutions, contracts and certifications relating to the hiring of Job Orders as provided for in Section 77 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: A copy of SP Resolution on hired Job Orders and contracts of service for Executive Assistants and other JO personnel were submitted to COA. Monthly contracts on hiring of JO personnel were also submitted.
observations.reason: Accomplishment Reports were already attached to DV/Payroll Payments.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct all concerned officials to submit copies of contracts and all their supporting documents, including those required in the auditorial review to the Auditor’s Office within five (5) calendar days from approval thereof for review/evaluation purposes pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Best effort is being undertaken to submit copies of the contracts and supporting documents to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Reiterated in AOM No. 2017-015 dated January 27, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the City Treasurer to transfer the deposits maintained with the Postal Bank to authorized government depository bank pursuant to the aforestated regulation.
observations.managementAction: Account with Postal Bank Dagupan City amounting to P157,134.46 was closed on April 1, 2016. Proceeds of closed PPSB deposited to LBP Alaminos on April 6, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management: • require the City Treasurer’s Office to submit the Report of Checks Issued and Report of Collection and Deposits to the Accounting Office within the prescribed time pursuant to Section 43 on the manual on NGAS; • instruct the City Accountant record the financial transactions immediately in the books of accounts and submit the reports including paid vouchers and receipts, to the Auditor within the first ten days of the ensuing month pursuant to the aforestated regulations.
observations.managementAction: Forwarding of RCI and RCD being done by the Office of the City Treasurer daily. Preparation of Journal Entry Vouchers and Journal Recording being done by the Office of the City Accountant daily.
observations.reason: Adhere to the provisions of Section 43 of NGAS Manual.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive make proper representation with the concerned Department of Education officials and seek their approval for the Hundred Islands E-Kawayan Factory to supply the requirements of its public elementary and secondary schools for their tables, chairs, furniture and building materials in accordance with DepEd’s technical specifications in order that its objectives could be efficiently and effectively achieved and more income would be generated from its operations.
observations.managementAction: MOA has not been established between the LGU and DepEd for the possible supply of furniture like tables and chairs to schools coming from the E-Kawayan Project. Nevertheless, the Office of the City Agriculturist had confirmed the possible agreement between the two agencies and the factory had already in the process of making chairs for distribution to schools.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management remit the unexpended balances of PDAF covered by the permanent injunction of Supreme Court to the National Treasury pursuant to the aforestated regulation.
observations.managementAction: The total amount of P5,653,116.27 was reverted back to the National Treasury on December 27, 2016.
observations.reason: Refund of unexpended balance of PDAF in the amount of P5,653,116.27 was supported by OR No. 2544962 dated December 27, 2016 (LBP Check No. 670880 dated 12/15/2016)
